pr.title: Mark ft_0CD1 as matching, run ninja apply, fix splits.txt
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Marked melee/ft/ft_0CD1.c as Matching in configure.py and applied the resulting generated/config updates. The PR also corrected ftdevice.c OSReport strings from a shared "coll func" message to the more specific "wind func" and "catch func" messages needed for the matching build, renamed two item split entries to their discovered Yoshi-related source names, and accepted a ninja-apply symbol rename for a local sdata2 float.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_0CD1.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ft_0CD1.c").
Updated src/melee/ft/ftdevice.c so ftCo_800C06E8 reports "fighter chk device wind func num over!" instead of "fighter chk device coll func num over!" before asserting.
Updated src/melee/ft/ftdevice.c so ftCo_800C0764 reports "fighter chk device catch func num over!" instead of "fighter chk device coll func num over!" before asserting.
Fixed config/GALE01/splits.txt item source labels: melee/it/items/it_2F2B.c became melee/it/items/ityoshitongue.c, and melee/it/items/it_27CF.c became melee/it/items/ityoshiegglay.c.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt at .sdata2:0x804D8EF8 from ftCo_804D8EF8 to the local autogenerated label @183, consistent with applying generated symbols after the matching change.
smart_moves: The PR did not only flip ft_0CD1.c to Matching; it also included the small string literal corrections in ftdevice.c that likely made the object byte-match.
The author ran the project generation/apply flow after marking the object matching, capturing related symbols.txt and splits.txt fallout in the same PR rather than leaving config drift.
The OSReport messages were made semantically specific to the code paths: ftCo_800C06E8 uses the wind-function overflow message and ftCo_800C0764 uses the catch-function overflow message.
The splits.txt corrections replaced anonymous address-style item file names with meaningful Yoshi item names, improving future navigation without changing section ranges.
decomp_lessons: For decomp matching, assert/report string literals are part of the binary evidence; even a plausible reused error string can prevent a match if the original had distinct text.
After moving a file from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, run the repository's apply/regeneration workflow and commit any symbols.txt or splits.txt updates it produces.

Generated local labels such as @183 in symbols.txt may replace named sdata2 symbols when the matching/generated symbol pipeline determines a symbol is local or compiler-generated; preserve generated output unless there is stronger naming evidence.
Split-file source names can be corrected independently of address ranges; meaningful file labels like ityoshitongue.c and ityoshiegglay.c help align config with actual item identities.
naming_conventions: Fighter common functions in this area use the ftCo_ prefix, as shown by ftCo_800C06E8 and ftCo_800C0764.
Unidentified fighter source units may retain address-style names such as ft_0CD1.c until better names are known.
Item source files should prefer semantic lowercase item names when identified, for example ityoshitongue.c and ityoshiegglay.c instead of it_2F2B.c and it_27CF.c.
Autogenerated/local sdata2 float symbols can use numbered labels like @183 rather than a manually named ftCo_804D8EF8 symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use exact string literal recovery as a low-level matching tactic: the PR changed only the OSReport text in two ftdevice.c paths but that was important enough to mark ft_0CD1.c matching.
When a matching toggle is made in configure.py, validate by running the build/apply workflow so object status, symbol names, and split metadata stay synchronized.
Do not assume repeated assert-report blocks share the same message; inspect each callsite's original literal or generated diff.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to the counts, but no comment body was included in the slice, so no substantive review feedback can be extracted.
searchable_terms: fighter chk device wind func num over
fighter chk device catch func num over
fighter chk device coll func num over
follow_up_queries: Find other ftdevice.c OSReport strings that still use generic or copied text and compare against original literals.
Search for remaining NonMatching fighter objects adjacent to ft_0CD1.c in configure.py that may only need small literal or symbol fixes.
Audit config/GALE01/splits.txt for remaining item files with address-style names like it_XXXX.c that have since been identified semantically.
Check whether ft_0CD1.c should eventually receive a semantic filename once its role is understood, since this PR only marked it matching.
